name: Felix
context: chemistry grad student abroad; threads: lab experiments, bouldering gym, visa renewal

episodes.text: Calibrated the new spectrometer all afternoon and logged baseline readings for next week's runs. Left lab at eight with the playlist still stuck in my head.

probes.query: Which instrument did I calibrate all afternoon?
I feel neutral, almost cozy, thinking of that routine calibration day.
An unhurried workday of dials and numbers hums back to me sometimes.

episodes.text: Third failed synthesis in a row, same yellow sludge instead of white crystals. My supervisor shrugged and said welcome to chemistry, which did not help at midnight.

probes.query: What did I get instead of white crystals in the failed synthesis?
I still feel frustrated recalling that string of failed reactions.
The same wrong color at the bottom of the flask, night after night, haunts my benchwork.

episodes.text: First time at the bouldering gym with Priya from the lab. Fell off the easiest wall four times, chalked my entire face somehow, signed up for a monthly pass anyway.

probes.query: How many times did I fall off the easiest wall at the gym?
I feel cheerful embarrassment thinking of my first climbing attempt.
Powder everywhere it shouldn't be and a floor that kept catching me — that visit resurfaces.

episodes.text: Visa renewal notice arrived: my appointment is in June and the checklist has eleven documents, two needing apostilles from home. Started a folder and a headache.

probes.query: How many documents are on the visa renewal checklist?
I feel anxious every time that visa checklist crosses my mind.
The envelope that decides whether I can stay keeps hovering over my plans.

episodes.text: Laundry day plus a bulk-cook of pasta sauce in the shared kitchen. Traded a jar of it with my flatmate for half his lasagna, an objectively great deal.

probes.query: What did I trade a jar of sauce for with my flatmate?
I feel lightly content about that kitchen barter evening.
A fair swap over the stove with the guy across the hall drifts back to me.

episodes.text: The synthesis worked. White needles under the scope, ninety-two percent purity on the first pass, and I did a silent victory lap around the empty lab at ten at night.

probes.query: What purity did the successful synthesis reach on the first pass?
I feel elated all over again when that successful reaction comes to mind.
Running a quiet lap between the benches with nobody watching still fizzes in me.

episodes.text: Sent my first-author manuscript draft to the group for comments. Twenty-three pages, four figures I remade six times, and one acknowledgment line thanking the night bus.

probes.query: How many pages was the manuscript draft I circulated?
I feel proud and nervous remembering sending that draft out.
Months of work compressed into one attachment left my hands that day and it stays with me.

episodes.text: Video call home for Mom's birthday. The connection froze on everyone mid-song, and Dad's cake had my childhood nickname iced on it by mistake, which nobody explained.

probes.query: What was iced on the cake by mistake during Mom's birthday call?
I feel a homesick ache thinking of that frozen birthday call.
A screen full of my people stuck mid-note, an ocean away, tugs at me on quiet evenings.

episodes.text: Sent my first purple-graded boulder problem after three weeks of trying. Priya filmed it, the whole gym section clapped, and I bought celebratory dumplings on the way home.

probes.query: What did I buy on the way home after sending the purple problem?
I feel triumphant remembering finally topping that climb.
Three weeks of falling ended in one clean line to the top, and it plays back often.

episodes.text: Quiet Sunday: fixed my bike's rear brake, watched a documentary about lighthouses, and answered two emails from undergrads about the lab rotation.

probes.query: Which part of my bike did I fix that Sunday?
I feel calm thinking of that slow documentary Sunday.
An easy day of small repairs and sea towers on the screen floats back to me.

episodes.text: Visa appointment day. The officer flipped through my folder, asked one question about funding, stamped the extension, and it was over in eleven minutes after three months of dread.

probes.query: How long did the visa appointment actually take?
I feel washed with relief when that appointment day comes back to me.
The stamp that let me exhale after a whole season echoes in my chest.

episodes.text: Restocked lab consumables, updated the group wiki page on the spectrometer quirks, and defrosted the ancient freezer nobody admits to owning.

probes.query: Which appliance did I defrost that nobody admits to owning?
I feel mildly satisfied about that lab housekeeping day.
An afternoon of unglamorous upkeep for the common good drifts through my mind.

episodes.text: Reviewer comments came back on the manuscript: major revision, fourteen points, two experiments to redo. Priya slid a dumpling across the table before I even said anything.

probes.query: How many points did the reviewers raise on my manuscript?
I feel deflated thinking about that revision verdict.
The list that sent me back to the bench sits in my drawer and in my head.

episodes.text: Groceries, a phone call with my sister about her new puppy, and an early gym session where I re-climbed the purple problem just to prove it wasn't luck.

probes.query: What did my sister and I talk about on the phone?
I feel light and content recalling that ordinary full day.
Doing the hard thing a second time, just for myself, comes back when I doubt.
